No Poverty.reason: While multinational finance can indirectly impact economic development, the course lacks explicit content or direct targets related to poverty eradication or specific pro-poor financial services. The link is very tenuous.

Zero Hunger.reason: The course content, focusing on multinational financial management, has no direct or indirect relevance to ending hunger, improving nutrition, or sustainable agriculture practices.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course syllabus contains no explicit or implicit content related to health, well-being, disease prevention, or access to healthcare services.

Quality Education.reason: The course provides quality tertiary education, equipping students with advanced professional financial knowledge and skills for employment, aligning directly with Targets 4.3 and 4.4.
evidence: Students will acquire advanced professional knowledge in finance
Students will develop creativity, communication skills and a global perspective

Gender Equality.reason: The course syllabus contains no explicit or implicit content, objectives, or topics related to gender equality or women's empowerment.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: The course content has no direct or indirect relevance to clean water, sanitation, water resource management, or pollution reduction.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: The course syllabus does not contain any content related to affordable and clean energy, renewable energy, or energy efficiency.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course directly addresses multinational financial management, international financing, and foreign direct investment, which are core drivers of economic growth (Target 8.1) and strengthen financial institutions (Target 8.10).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course's focus on global financing channels and costs for multinational enterprises directly supports industrialization and infrastructure development by facilitating access to financial services (Targets 9.1, 9.2, 9.3).
evidence: 多國籍企業全球融資管道 (Global financing channels for multinational enterprises)
多國籍企業融資成本 (Cost of financing for multinational enterprises)

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course examines global financial markets, international monetary systems, and foreign direct investment, which are relevant to regulating financial institutions (Target 10.5) and enhancing developing countries' voice in global governance (Target 10.6).

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: The course content does not address sustainable cities, urban planning, housing, transport, or community development.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: Core competency A explicitly states students will 'understand and be able to identify... sustainability considerations of business situations,' directly supporting Target 12.6 for multinational companies.
evidence: understand and be able to identify the ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability considerations of business situations

Climate Action.reason: The course syllabus does not contain any explicit or implicit content related to climate change, its impacts, or actions to combat it.

Life Below Water.reason: The course content has no direct or indirect relevance to marine ecosystems, ocean health, or sustainable use of marine resources.

Life on Land.reason: The course content has no direct or indirect relevance to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, forests, or land degradation.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course explicitly covers corporate governance (Target 16.6), political risk assessment, and financial malpractice, which are crucial for developing accountable institutions and reducing illicit financial flows (Target 16.4).
